paper: Proximal Policy Optimization Algorithms (Schulman et al., 2017)

artifacts.label: Original PPO paper (arXiv preprint)
artifacts.version_clues: Submitted July 2017; no versioned releases
artifacts.risk_notes: Canonical reference; no code here but defines all hyperparameters used for reproduction

artifacts.label: openai/baselines — Official OpenAI implementation (TensorFlow)
artifacts.version_clues: pposgd (ppo1) first committed 2017-07-20 (commit da99706); ppo2 introduced 2017-11-16 (commit 2dd7d30); final stable ppo2 commit ea25b9e (2020-01-31). Requires TensorFlow 1.x (1.4–1.14), gym>=0.15.4,<0.16.0, mujoco-py 0.5.x–1.50, Python 3.5+
artifacts.risk_notes: TF1 ecosystem is EOL; mujoco-py version sensitivity is very high; MuJoCo license was required (now free via DeepMind); repo is in maintenance-only mode; Windows install known to fail. ppo2 and ppo1 differ in architecture (GPU-vectorised vs MPI). Use commit ea25b9e for exact ppo2 hyperparameters.

artifacts.label: openai/baselines ppo2/defaults.py — authoritative hyperparameter file
artifacts.version_clues: Extracted MuJoCo defaults: nsteps=2048, nminibatches=32, lam=0.95, gamma=0.99, noptepochs=10, ent_coef=0.0, lr=3e-4, cliprange=0.2. Atari defaults: nsteps=128, nminibatches=4, lam=0.95, gamma=0.99, noptepochs=4, ent_coef=0.01, lr=2.5e-4, cliprange=0.1
artifacts.risk_notes: Some Atari defaults diverge slightly from Table 5 of the paper (paper: K=3 epochs, noptepochs=4 in code). Annealing schedules are baked into lr= lambda expressions.

artifacts.label: openai/spinningup — Educational PPO reference (TF + PyTorch)
artifacts.version_clues: Last commit 2020-01-30; PyTorch branch added January 2020; Python >=3.6; TensorFlow 1.x or PyTorch; MPI-based parallelism
artifacts.risk_notes: In maintenance mode only; MPI dependency adds environment complexity; focuses on PPO-Clip variant which matches paper. No Atari wrapper included — MuJoCo-only coverage.

artifacts.label: CleanRL — High-quality single-file PPO (PyTorch, actively maintained)
artifacts.version_clues: PyTorch >=1.12.1; Python >=3.7.1,<3.11; gymnasium migration ongoing; v1.0.0 released 2022; ppo.py (discrete), ppo_continuous_action.py (MuJoCo), ppo_atari.py (Atari). Uses gymnasium v4 MuJoCo envs (≈v2 performance). Requirements split: requirements/requirements-atari.txt, requirements/requirements-mujoco.txt
artifacts.risk_notes: Uses gymnasium (not gym) — environment version difference from paper (v1 → v4 envs). Paper used -v1 MuJoCo envs; CleanRL uses -v4. Benchmark validated: ~400 episodic return on Breakout. Most actively maintained correct reference.

artifacts.label: vwxyzjn/ppo-implementation-details — Source code for '37 Implementation Details of PPO' blog post
artifacts.version_clues: Companion to ICLR 2022 blog post; references openai/baselines ppo2 commit ea25b9e as canonical; step-by-step reconstructions of all 37 implementation tricks
artifacts.risk_notes: Excellent forensic resource for understanding what the paper omits. Documents critical tricks: orthogonal init, value clipping, advantage normalization per minibatch, global gradient clipping (0.5), Adam eps=1e-5. Not directly runnable as a standalone benchmark.

artifacts.label: ikostrikov/pytorch-a2c-ppo-acktr-gail — Early PyTorch PPO reference (2017)
artifacts.version_clues: First commit October 2017 (archived snapshot: 2017-10-14); uses same hyperparameters as openai/baselines; supports MuJoCo, Atari, PyBullet, DM Control; Internet Archive snapshot available
artifacts.risk_notes: Legacy repo; may use older gym APIs. Well-known in RL community as the canonical PyTorch PPO reference prior to CleanRL. Many later libraries (SB3, CleanRL) inherit from it.

artifacts.label: DLR-RM/stable-baselines3 — Production PyTorch PPO (actively maintained)
artifacts.version_clues: v2.9.0a2 current; Python 3.10+; PyTorch; gymnasium support; uses -v4 MuJoCo envs; pip install stable-baselines3[extra] for Atari + MuJoCo
artifacts.risk_notes: Inherits from openai/baselines ppo2 and ikostrikov repo. clip_range=0.1 recommended for unstable envs. Atari: use make_atari_env helper. Not verbatim paper reproduction but closest maintained library.

artifacts.label: Papers With Code — PPO paper page with code links and benchmarks
artifacts.version_clues: Lists all community implementations with star counts and framework tags; benchmark tables for MuJoCo and Atari from multiple reimplementations
artifacts.risk_notes: Redirects to HuggingFace paper page (302); check both URLs. Good for discovering additional reimplementations.

artifacts.label: Papers With Code — PPO method page
artifacts.version_clues: Method overview, linked datasets (MuJoCo, Atari ALE), benchmark leaderboards
artifacts.risk_notes: Use as secondary discovery source for additional implementations and benchmark scores.

artifacts.label: openai/mujoco-py official Dockerfile (CUDA base)
artifacts.version_clues: MuJoCo 1.50 and 1.31; CUDA base image; headless rendering setup; relevant for GPU-based MuJoCo training
artifacts.risk_notes: mujoco-py is deprecated in favour of dm_control / mujoco (Google DeepMind); MuJoCo license now free but binary download URL changed. Use mujoco>=2.x with gymnasium for modern reproduction.

artifacts.label: deep-rl-docker — Docker image with OpenAI Gym, Baselines, MuJoCo, Roboschool, TensorFlow
artifacts.version_clues: Bundles MuJoCo 1.50+1.31, Roboschool, TF1, OpenAI Gym, Baselines; JupyterLab interface; GPU-enabled
artifacts.risk_notes: Community image, not official. Roboschool is included, matching paper Section 6.3. TF1-based; check TF version compatibility with baselines ppo1/ppo2.

artifacts.label: OpenAI Gym — MuJoCo continuous control environments (HalfCheetah, Hopper, etc.)
artifacts.version_clues: Paper uses -v1 suffix environments (e.g. HalfCheetah-v1); gym>=0.9.x for -v1 support; modern gym/gymnasium uses -v4; pip install gym mujoco-py
artifacts.risk_notes: MuJoCo-v1 environments are deprecated. Exact -v1 dynamics differ from -v2/-v4. For faithful paper reproduction, pin gym==0.9.x with mujoco-py==0.5.7. For practical reproduction, -v4 with gymnasium is acceptable with noted performance delta.

artifacts.label: OpenAI Gym repository — Atari ALE wrapper and MuJoCo envs
artifacts.version_clues: Atari via ale-py + ROMs; pip install gym[atari]; 49 Atari games available at time of paper; NoopResetEnv, MaxAndSkipEnv, EpisodicLifeEnv, FireResetEnv, WarpFrame, ClipRewardEnv, FrameStack wrappers required (from baselines/common/atari_wrappers.py)
artifacts.risk_notes: Gym maintenance transferred to Farama Foundation (gymnasium). ROM licensing changed — use pip install gymnasium[atari,accept-rom-license]. -v1 MuJoCo envs removed in gym>=0.26.

artifacts.label: OpenAI Roboschool — RoboschoolHumanoid, Flagrun, FlagrunHarder environments
artifacts.version_clues: Used in Section 6.3; pip install roboschool (deprecated); PyBullet-based; now superseded by pybullet-gym or similar
artifacts.risk_notes: Roboschool is officially deprecated. pip install roboschool no longer works on modern Python. Use pybullet or PyBullet-Gym as replacement. Reproducing Section 6.3 results is high-risk.

artifacts.label: Hugging Face Hub — Stable-Baselines3 pre-trained PPO checkpoints (MuJoCo, Atari)
artifacts.version_clues: SB3 models uploaded to HF Hub; MuJoCo HalfCheetah, Hopper, Walker2d, Reacher, Ant, etc.; Atari game checkpoints; load with sb3.PPO.load()
artifacts.risk_notes: These are SB3 checkpoints, not original paper checkpoints. Useful for baseline comparison but not faithful to Schulman 2017 exact training setup.

artifacts.label: '37 Implementation Details of PPO' — Definitive reproduction guide (Costa Huang, 2022)
artifacts.version_clues: References ppo2 commit ea25b9e as canonical; documents all 37 tricks including orthogonal init, value clipping, per-minibatch advantage normalization, Adam eps=1e-5, global grad clip 0.5, lr annealing; Atari wrappers enumerated
artifacts.risk_notes: Essential reading before any PPO reproduction attempt. Identifies 13 core + 9 Atari + 9 MuJoCo + 5 LSTM + 1 MultiDiscrete implementation tricks absent from the paper. Validation target: ~400 return on Breakout at 10M steps.

artifacts.label: openai/baselines Issue #625 — MuJoCo/gym version compatibility question
artifacts.version_clues: Reports gym+mujoco-py version confusion; users report FlattenDictWrapper import errors with gym==0.9.3, mujoco-py==0.5.7
artifacts.risk_notes: High-signal reproduction risk indicator. Version pinning is critical: gym==0.15.x with mujoco-py==1.50 is the stable combination for baselines ppo2.

artifacts.label: openai/baselines Issue #7 — Requirements not clearly stated
artifacts.version_clues: Early user reports of unclear TF/gym/mujoco-py version requirements; confirms absence of pinned requirements.txt
artifacts.risk_notes: Historical signal: baselines never had a requirements.txt with pinned versions, making exact environment recreation hard.

artifacts.label: openai/baselines ppo2.py at commit ea25b9e — Authoritative frozen snapshot
artifacts.version_clues: Commit ea25b9e (2020-01-31) is the final stable version of ppo2 identified by the '37 details' blog as the canonical implementation; TF1; vectorized envs
artifacts.risk_notes: Pin to this exact commit for reproduction fidelity. Subsequent commits may introduce breaking changes.

recommended_repo_rationale: CleanRL ppo_continuous_action.py (MuJoCo) and ppo_atari.py (Atari) are the most actively maintained, well-documented, and benchmarked faithful implementations of PPO. They incorporate all 37 implementation details, use PyTorch (avoiding TF1 EOL issues), and are validated against reference results. For strict -v1 environment fidelity, pair with openai/baselines ppo2 at commit ea25b9e on a legacy TF1 Docker image.

dataset_links.name: OpenAI Gym (MuJoCo -v1 environments)
dataset_links.note: Paper uses HalfCheetah-v1, Hopper-v1, InvertedDoublePendulum-v1, InvertedPendulum-v1, Reacher-v1, Swimmer-v1, Walker2d-v1. -v1 is removed in gym>=0.26; pin to gym==0.15.x for exact reproduction.
dataset_links.modern_alternative: pip install gymnasium[mujoco]  # uses -v4 envs, slight dynamics difference

dataset_links.name: Atari ALE (49 games via OpenAI Gym)
dataset_links.note: ROMs now auto-accepted via ale-py license flag. Paper uses 49 games for 40M game frames (10M timesteps) each. Requires atari_wrappers.py preprocessing (NoopReset, MaxAndSkip, EpisodicLife, FireReset, WarpFrame 84x84, ClipReward, FrameStack-4).

dataset_links.name: Roboschool (Section 6.3 only, qualitative)
dataset_links.install: DEPRECATED — use pybullet-gym or PyBullet as replacement
dataset_links.note: RoboschoolHumanoid-v0, RoboschoolHumanoidFlagrun-v0, RoboschoolHumanoidFlagrunHarder-v0. Section 6.3 is qualitative only — no quantitative comparison to baseline methods. Lowest priority for reproduction.

dependency_clues: Python: 3.5–3.8 for openai/baselines TF1; 3.7–3.10 for CleanRL/SB3
TensorFlow: 1.4–1.14 for openai/baselines ppo1/ppo2 (TF1 only, EOL)
PyTorch: >=1.12.1 for CleanRL; any recent version for ikostrikov/SB3
gym: ==0.15.7 for -v1 MuJoCo envs with baselines; use gymnasium>=0.26 for modern reproduction
mujoco-py: ==1.50.1.68 for TF1-era baselines (MuJoCo 1.50 binary + license required); OR use mujoco>=2.3 with gymnasium
ale-py + ROMs: pip install gymnasium[atari,accept-rom-license] for Atari
OpenMPI: required for openai/baselines ppo1 (MPI-based parallelism) and spinningup
Key ppo2 Adam settings: eps=1e-5 (NOT PyTorch default 1e-8 — critical!); lr=3e-4 MuJoCo, 2.5e-4 Atari
Orthogonal init: hidden layers scale=sqrt(2), policy head scale=0.01, value head scale=1.0 (NOT default PyTorch init)
Global gradient clipping: max_norm=0.5 (absent from paper, present in all reference implementations)
Value function loss clipping: enabled in ppo2 (also absent from paper text but present in code)
Per-minibatch advantage normalization: normalize advantages within each minibatch, not the full rollout batch
Docker: use eric-heiden/deep-rl-docker for all-in-one TF1 + MuJoCo + Roboschool; or build custom with nvidia/cuda:10.0 + TF1.14 + mujoco-py 1.50

reproduction_risk_summary.mujoco_v1_exact: HIGH — requires pinned gym==0.15.x + mujoco-py 1.50 + TF1; -v1 envs removed from modern gym
reproduction_risk_summary.mujoco_v4_approximate: LOW — gymnasium + CleanRL ppo_continuous_action.py works out of box; minor dynamics difference from -v1
reproduction_risk_summary.atari_49games: MEDIUM — Atari ROM licensing resolved; 37 implementation tricks required for correct results; wrapper stack critical
reproduction_risk_summary.roboschool_section63: VERY HIGH — Roboschool deprecated; qualitative-only section with no quantitative baselines; skip for primary reproduction
